name: La Vall de la Gallinera — Circular from Pego to the top of Miserat
name_original: La Vall de la Gallinera — Circular desde Pego hasta la cima del Miserat
region_intro: Road cycling around Pego, located in Spain's Marina Alta region, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The area is characterized by a network of winding roads that traverse between the Mediterranean coast and mountainous inland areas, including the Sierra de Segària and Vall de Gallinera. Routes feature varied elevation profiles, from gentle gradients through wetlands and rice fields to challenging ascents over 1,000 meters in elevation gain. This geographical mix provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Pego The most popular road cycling route is La Vall de la Gallinera — Circular from Pego to the top of Miserat, a 37.2 miles (59.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain, climbing towards the top of Miserat. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of the Sierra de Segària – Pego–Sagra Road loop from Pego, a moderate 29.3 miles (47.1 km) path. This route offers views of the Sierra de Segària and includes a moderate ascent. Local road cyclists also love the Dénia to Gandía Greenway – Les Magazinos, Dénia loop from Pego, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas and greenways, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Road cycling around Pego is defined by winding roads through valleys, views of mountain ranges, and paths near wetlands and rice fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Pego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 4,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on Pego's road cycling routes?
A: Pego offers a diverse range of terrain, from gentle gradients through wetlands and rice fields to challenging ascents over 1,000 meters in elevation gain. You'll find winding panoramic roads that traverse between the Mediterranean coast and mountainous inland areas, including the Sierra de Segària and Vall de Gallinera. Routes can feature significant climbs, such as the one towards the top of Miserat on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891652689/la-vall-de-la-gallinera-circular-from-pego-to-the-top-of-miserat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall de la Gallinera — Circular from Pego to the top of Miserat&lt;/a&gt; route, or more moderate ascents like those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Segària – Pego–Sagra Road loop from Pego&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Pego has routes suitable for various skill levels, including easier options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31929359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia to Gandía Greenway – Les Magazinos, Dénia loop from Pego&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 26.9-mile (43.3 km) trail that leads through coastal areas and greenways with minimal elevation gain. The region also offers routes with gentle gradients and shaded sections, such as the road from Pego to Sagra, providing a quiet and engaging ride.

Q: What scenic views or attractions can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Pego offer stunning views of the sea, mountains, and countryside. You can expect to pass through charming Spanish villages and enjoy panoramic vistas. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4155453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley&lt;/a&gt;, known for its winding roads and breathtaking mountain views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/a&gt;, a famous mountain pass just a few kilometers away with spectacular panoramas. Many routes also offer views of the Sierra de Segària, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39549888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Sorell – View of the Sierra de Segària loop from Pego&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural parks accessible by road bike in the Pego area?
A: Yes, the Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park is a significant wetland area near Pego that is ideal for cycling. While primarily known for walking and birdwatching, its paths and rice fields offer unique, flat cycling opportunities. The region is also encircled by mountains like the Sierra de Segaria and Sierra de la Mariola, which provide scenic backdrops and more challenging routes.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Pego?
A: Absolutely. Many of the road cycling routes around Pego are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891652689/la-vall-de-la-gallinera-circular-from-pego-to-the-top-of-miserat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall de la Gallinera — Circular from Pego to the top of Miserat&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39539324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pego Town Center – Gallinera Valley loop from Pego&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the picturesque Gallinera Valley.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Pego?
A: Pego benefits from a sunny Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Professional cycling teams often choose this region for training, drawn by its favorable conditions. Peak cycling months are typically February and May, offering pleasant temperatures before the intense heat of summer and after the cooler winter months. However, the diverse routes mean you can find enjoyable rides in most seasons.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Pego?
A: The road cycling routes in Pego are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse terrain, from coastal greenways to challenging mountain passes, and the stunning panoramic views of the sea and mountains. The variety of routes catering to all skill levels and the charming villages encountered along the way are also often highlighted.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes suitable for children in Pego?
A: While many routes in Pego feature significant elevation changes, there are options that can be enjoyed by families, particularly those with older children or those comfortable with moderate distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31929359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dénia to Gandía Greenway – Les Magazinos, Dénia loop from Pego&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route with minimal elevation, making it a good choice for a more relaxed family outing. The Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park also offers flat paths suitable for a leisurely ride.

Q: Are there good places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the road cycling routes in Pego often connect picturesque Spanish villages, providing opportunities for refreshment stops. These charming towns typically have local cafes and restaurants where you can refuel and experience local culture. The town of Pego itself, with its old town and narrow medieval streets, is a great starting and ending point for rides, offering numerous options for food and drink.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling in Pego?
A: Pego, being a popular destination for outdoor activities, generally offers parking options within the town or near common starting points for routes. Specific parking areas may vary, but you can typically find public parking in the town center or designated areas for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most current information on parking availability and regulations.

Q: Is Pego accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: Pego is primarily a regional town in the Marina Alta area. While local bus services connect Pego to nearby towns, direct public transport options specifically catering to cyclists with bikes might be limited. For longer distances, combining train services to larger nearby cities like Gandía or Dénia and then cycling or using local transport to Pego might be an option, but it's best to verify bike carriage policies with specific transport providers.
tips: One of the most attractive and demanding stretches of road between La Safor and Vall d'Albaida. From Villalonga uphill to Llacuna, the road offers an entertaining zig-zag route with beautiful views in all directions.
Breathtakingly good descent. I imagine it would take your breath away getting up it as well.,

One of those summits that will amaze you with the views but getting there will test your preparation and physical resistance. With sections of sustained slope at 15%, you will have an interesting challenge that is more than rewarded by the impressive views of the entire valley and the coast.
